response.justification: The candidate is a strong full-stack engineer with deep TypeScript/Node.js expertise and solid software craftsmanship habits, but the role requires deep JVM mastery, Kotlin fluency, and hands-on Spring/enterprise framework experience — none of which are present in the resume. Java appears only in peripheral, non-enterprise contexts in older roles, and Kotlin is absent entirely. The closest relevant signal is a stint writing C/C++ components for NI LabWindows/CVI (a C IDE), which shows some IDE tooling exposure, but it is neither JVM-based nor at the scale or depth this position demands.
strengths: Prior IDE tooling contribution: wrote C/C++ components for NI LabWindows/CVI, a real engineering IDE product used globally
Strong software craftsmanship culture (TDD, trunk-based development, CI/CD) aligns with quality and reliability expectations of the role
Recent developer tooling work at re:cinq (MCP code generators, agentic workflow frameworks) shows genuine systems-level tooling interest
concerns: Kotlin is entirely absent from the resume — a hard requirement for this position
No Spring, Quarkus, Hibernate, or any JVM enterprise framework experience is evident anywhere
Primary production stack is TypeScript/Node.js; Java appears only in brief, non-enterprise, non-JVM-depth contexts

key_factors.factor: No Kotlin or Spring/JVM enterprise experience
IDE tooling exposure via NI LabWindows/CVI
TypeScript-first career with minimal Java depth
